title: Golden Crisp Portuguese Gouda Gratin
description: A one-pot Portuguese dish featuring crispy Gouda cheese and bitter namul, cooked to perfection in a robata grill, this gratin is a Mediterranean-friendly delight that combines the best of Portuguese and Asian flavors in a single vessel.
instructions: Preheat the robata grill to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, blanch the namul in boiling water for 30 seconds, then immediately submerge it in an ice bath to stop the cooking process.
In the same bowl, mix together the blanched namul, olive oil, garlic, lemon juice, salt, black pepper, and paprika.
In a separate bowl, mix together the shredded Gouda cheese and bread crumbs.
Add the cheese and bread crumb mixture to the namul mixture and stir until well combined.
Transfer the mixture to a robata grill vessel and top with additional bread crumbs and parsley.
Cook the gratin in the preheated robata grill for 20 minutes, or until the top is golden brown and the cheese is melted and bubbly.
Remove the gratin from the grill and let it cool for 5 minutes before serving.
Serve the gratin hot, garnished with additional parsley if desired.
